---
title: "nitrix.stats.connectivity"
generated: true
signature-hash: 79f42479388d09ad
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.connectivity`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `L` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `ebic_score` {#ebic_score}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:ebic_score -->
```python
ebic_score(theta: Float[Array, 'p p'], S: Float[Array, 'p p'], n: int | Float[Array, ''], *, gamma: float = 0.5, edge_tol: float = 1e-08) -> Float[Array, '']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:ebic_score -->
Extended Bayesian Information Criterion of a precision estimate.

`EBIC_gamma = n (tr(S Theta) - log det Theta) + E log n + 4 gamma E log p`
(Foygel & Drton 2010), where `E` is the number of off-diagonal edges
(upper-triangle entries with `|theta| > edge_tol`, counted once).  The
Gaussian-graphical-model term `-2 loglik = n (tr(S Theta) - log det Theta)`
uses the cuSOLVER-free rolled-Cholesky `log det`.  `gamma in [0, 1]`
tunes the extra sparsity prior (`gamma = 0` recovers plain BIC); pick the
path point with the **smallest** EBIC.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:ebic_score -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `theta` | `Float` | `p` × `p` | — | — |
| `S` | `Float` | `p` × `p` | — | — |
| `n` | `int | Float[Array, '']` | — | — | — |
| `gamma` | `float` | — | `0.5` | — |
| `edge_tol` | `float` | — | `1e-08` | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape —.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `glasso` {#glasso}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:glasso -->
```python
glasso(S: Float[Array, 'p p'], lam: float | Float[Array, ''], *, n_outer: int = 100, n_inner: int = 50) -> Float[Array, 'p p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:glasso -->
Graphical-LASSO sparse precision (inverse covariance).

Solves `argmin_Theta <S, Theta> - log det Theta + lam ||Theta||_{1,off}`
(off-diagonal-only L1 penalty -- the standard / nilearn convention) by
Friedman/Hastie/Tibshirani (2008) block-coordinate descent: an outer sweep
over columns, each an inner lasso on the working covariance `W`, with the
precision recovered from the converged per-column coefficients.  No
factorisation, no cuSOLVER; all loops rolled (`O(p^2)` graph), so it is
differentiable through the fixed iteration budget and compiles flat in `p`
(fine for connectome `p = 100-400`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:glasso -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `S` | `Float` | `p` × `p` | — | `(p, p)` sample covariance (e.g. `stats.covariance.cov` or a shrinkage estimate). Off-diagonal-only penalty fixes `W_jj = S_jj`. |
| `lam` | `float | Float[Array, '']` | — | — | Non-negative L1 penalty. Larger `lam` -> sparser `Theta`. |
| `n_outer` | `int` | — | `100` | Outer column sweeps and inner lasso sweeps per column (fixed budget; the defaults reach KKT stationarity to machine precision on well-scaled connectome covariances and can be reduced for speed). |
| `n_inner` | `int` | — | `50` | Outer column sweeps and inner lasso sweeps per column (fixed budget; the defaults reach KKT stationarity to machine precision on well-scaled connectome covariances and can be reduced for speed). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `p` × `p`. `(p, p)` symmetric sparse precision; the inactive off-diagonals are exactly zero (the conditional-independence graph).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `glasso_path` {#glasso_path}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:glasso_path -->
```python
glasso_path(S: Float[Array, 'p p'], lambdas: Float[Array, 'L'], *, n_outer: int = 100, n_inner: int = 50) -> Float[Array, 'L p p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:glasso_path -->
Warm-started graphical-LASSO regularisation path over `lambdas`.

Sweeps the penalties in the **given order**, carrying the working covariance
`W` and the lasso coefficients `B` from one `lam` to the next so each
solve starts warm (far cheaper than a cold restart and the usual way to
trace a glasso path -- pass `lambdas` descending, dense -> sparse, or
ascending; either direction warm-starts).  Returns the stacked precisions
aligned with `lambdas`; pair with :func:`ebic_score` for model selection.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:glasso_path -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `S` | `Float` | `p` × `p` | — | — |
| `lambdas` | `Float` | `L` | — | — |
| `n_outer` | `int` | — | `100` | — |
| `n_inner` | `int` | — | `50` | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `L` × `p` × `p`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `ledoit_wolf` {#ledoit_wolf}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:ledoit_wolf -->
```python
ledoit_wolf(X: Float[Array, 'n p'], *, assume_centered: bool = False) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:ledoit_wolf -->
Ledoit-Wolf analytic-shrinkage covariance.

Returns `(cov, shrinkage)`.  The shrinkage intensity is
`alpha = beta^2 / delta^2` with `delta^2 = ||S - mu I||_F^2` and
`beta^2 = (1/n^2) sum_k ||x_k||^4 - (1/n) ||S||_F^2` clipped to
`[0, delta^2]` (Ledoit & Wolf 2004).  Matches
`sklearn.covariance.ledoit_wolf`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:ledoit_wolf -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `X` | `Float` | `n` × `p` | — | — |
| `assume_centered` | `bool` | — | `False` | — |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `oas` {#oas}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:oas -->
```python
oas(X: Float[Array, 'n p'], *, assume_centered: bool = False) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:oas -->
Oracle-Approximating-Shrinkage covariance (Chen et al. 2010).

Returns `(cov, shrinkage)`.  Same convex blend as Ledoit-Wolf, a
different closed-form `alpha`.  Matches `sklearn.covariance.oas`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:oas -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `X` | `Float` | `n` × `p` | — | — |
| `assume_centered` | `bool` | — | `False` | — |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `shrunk_covariance` {#shrunk_covariance}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:shrunk_covariance -->
```python
shrunk_covariance(X: Float[Array, 'n p'], *, method: ShrinkageMethod = 'ledoit_wolf', assume_centered: bool = False) -> Float[Array, 'p p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:shrunk_covariance -->
Analytic-shrinkage covariance via `method` (the cov only).

`'ledoit_wolf'` (default, nilearn's connectome default) or `'oas'`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:shrunk_covariance -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `X` | `Float` | `n` × `p` | — | — |
| `method` | `ShrinkageMethod` | — | `'ledoit_wolf'` | — |
| `assume_centered` | `bool` | — | `False` | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `p` × `p`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
